title: Framework Benchmark Constraint
description: A single non-negotiable constraint that any candidate unified-physics framework must satisfy. Together, the entries in benchmarks/ form a falsifier set: a framework that fails any one of them is dead.

id.description: Stable slug used as the filename.

kind.description: reduction = must reproduce an established theory in a stated regime; precision_test = must reproduce a specific measurement to its quoted precision; principle_invariance = must respect a foundational symmetry to current experimental bound; forbidden_phenomenon = must not predict a phenomenon already excluded; cosmological_observable = must reproduce a cosmological observation; tension_to_address = links to existing data/ entries that the framework must explain; pattern_compression = the structured set of values within an apparently 'ugly' observable should compress to a smaller number of inputs under the right ansatz (e.g., quark mass spectrum as integer Froggatt-Nielsen charges).

requirement.description: Plain-English statement of what any candidate framework must do to pass this benchmark.

current_bound.description: Current best measurement or upper limit. Required for precision_test, principle_invariance, forbidden_phenomenon, cosmological_observable.

recovered_theory.description: For 'reduction' kind: which established theory must be reproduced (e.g. 'General Relativity', 'QED', 'Standard Model', 'LambdaCDM').

limit.description: For 'reduction' kind: the regime where the recovery is required (e.g. 'weak field, low velocity', 'energies << 1 TeV').

principle.description: For 'principle_invariance' kind: which principle is being tested.

tension_links.description: For 'tension_to_address' kind: list of data/<id> entries that any new framework must explain.

what_it_excludes.description: Plain-English statement of which frameworks or model classes are killed by this benchmark, so it is clear why this constraint matters.

procedural.description: How a candidate framework would be evaluated against this benchmark. Text-first: each entry should name the canonical parameterization (PPN, SME, SMEFT, ΛCDM extensions, etc.), state the predicted observable in that parameterization, give the numerical bound in those parameters, and list what a candidate framework must expose to be evaluated. This makes the constraint executable in principle even before evaluator code is written.

name.description: Canonical parameterization (e.g. 'Parameterized Post-Newtonian (PPN)', 'Standard Model Effective Field Theory (Warsaw basis)', 'Standard Model Extension (SME) photon sector').

predicted_observable.description: Formula or expression giving the experimentally measured observable in terms of the parameterization.

bound_in_parameterization.description: The numerical bound restated in the parameterization's variables.

framework_inputs.description: Concrete things a candidate framework must expose (compute, derive, predict) to be evaluated against this benchmark.

evaluator_status.description: How hard the evaluation is: 'trivial' (single-number compare), 'tractable' (extract a parameter from a closed-form computation), 'requires_code' (PPN extraction, SMEFT matching), 'requires_simulation' (Boltzmann hierarchy through recombination, lattice QCD), 'research_problem' (no general method exists yet).

compression_ansatz.description: For 'pattern_compression' kind: the functional form that the apparently random data is hypothesised to fit (e.g., 'm_i ~ v * eps^{q_i} with integer q_i and single eps'). A mechanism passes the benchmark if it produces this ansatz from first principles.

target_data.description: For 'pattern_compression' kind: the observables whose pattern is being compressed.

free_parameters.description: For 'pattern_compression' kind: the small number of parameters the ansatz fits, against which compression is measured.
